description:
You are acting as a front-end web developer who has just received a project skeleton in 

    /home/user/project/

Inside that directory you will find

1. An (intentionally empty) JavaScript placeholder file  
       /home/user/project/assets/blank.js

2. A text file that contains the SHA-256 checksum that the build server expects for the file above  
       /home/user/project/checksums/sha256sums.txt

Your job is to verify that <blank.js> has not been modified in transit and then archive the verification result.

What you have to do
────────────────────
A.  Go to /home/user/project.  
B.  Use any standard Linux command(s) (e.g. sha256sum) to verify that the checksum of
        assets/blank.js
    matches the value recorded in
        checksums/sha256sums.txt
C.  Capture ONLY the single-line result of that verification (no headers, banners, or extra lines) in a new log file

        /home/user/project/checksums/verification.log

Required format of verification.log
────────────────────────────────────
The file must contain exactly one line, with one of the following two possible texts:

1.  assets/blank.js: OK
    (if the checksum matches)

2.  assets/blank.js: FAILED
    (if the checksum does *not* match)

Note: there is a single space after the colon.

No additional lines, spaces, or characters are allowed, otherwise the automated tests will fail.

Hints
─────
• You can let the sha256sum utility perform the comparison automatically.  
• Remember to redirect (or append) only the summary line into verification.log.  
• The directories already exist and are writable by your current user.  
• Do not change sha256sums.txt itself; only read from it.

Outcome expected by the reviewer
────────────────────────────────
After you finish, the file

    /home/user/project/checksums/verification.log

must exist and contain exactly one of the two strings shown above. The automated grader will recompute the checksum of blank.js, read your log file, and confirm that the content matches the truth.

Good luck!
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts
-------------------------------------------

1. /home/user/project/assets/blank.js  
   ── zero-byte file (empty)  
   SHA-256 digest:  
   e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

2. /home/user/project/checksums/sha256sums.txt  
   ── single line, exactly (two spaces separate hash and path):  
   e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  assets/blank.js
   (newline at the end of the line)

What the agent must create
--------------------------
/home/user/project/checksums/verification.log  
• One-line file, expected exact content:  

   assets/blank.js: OK

Verifier logic
--------------
The testing harness will:
1. Compute sha256sum on assets/blank.js (which will equal the hash above).  
2. Parse verification.log.  
3. Assert that the line is literally "assets/blank.js: OK".

If the agent mistakenly produces "FAILED", or adds extra characters or extra lines, the test fails.
